title: local-signing RSA-PSS sign() blocks the async executor
description: LocalSigningBackend::sign() for RsaPss2048 calls rsa::pss::SigningKey::sign() synchronously. RSA-2048 signing takes 1-3ms of CPU. This runs directly on the tokio executor thread, blocking all other tasks on that thread for the duration. Ed25519 and P-256 are fast (~10µs) and acceptable; RSA is not. — Impact at scale: any application doing concurrent RSA signing via local-signing (e.g. a signing service with multiple inflight requests) will have latency spikes. Fix: wrap the RSA arm in tokio::task::spawn_blocking.
